cards.back: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability — the three pillars of information security
Social engineering attack using fraudulent messages to steal credentials or data
Multi-Factor Authentication — something you know, have, and are
Security model: never trust, always verify — no implicit trust by network location
Symmetric = one shared key (AES). Asymmetric = public/private key pair (RSA)
Distributed Denial of Service — flooding a target from many sources to make it unavailable
Users get only the minimum access needed to do their job
Security Information and Event Management — aggregates and analyzes security logs in real time
Malware that encrypts data and demands payment for the decryption key
Manipulating people, not systems, to gain access or information
